name: Ahmed
manager_notes: Ahmed talks about senior scope but often redirects into tactical delivery details. I want to understand whether this is temporary pressure or a deeper confidence gap around visibility and influence at his level. This growth conversation should surface how he defines the next-level role, what stakeholder trust gaps he sees, and where he is still operating as an individual executor. I need him to leave with a concrete growth path tied to strategic ownership, not another vague list of good habits.
answers: Wants bigger scope but still spends too much time in execution details.
When conversations get political, retreats to what he can deliver directly.
Can run projects, but hesitates on shaping cross-org alignment.
Partly confidence — doesn't want to look unprepared in senior forums.
Hasn't been explicit enough about what opportunities he wants.
To grow, needs to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.
Should delegate more hands-on work to create room for that.
A clear growth contract with milestones would keep him accountable.
Wants to own one strategic bet end-to-end, milestone check-in with stakeholders in two weeks.
